mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, consumer spending power contracts sharply, reducing corporate revenue growth expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0, or signal loses predictive power after controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which compounds corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward returns turn negative when both series rise concurrently for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which accelerates short-term corporate profit revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle phases, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which lifts equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses 2.0%, or the signal is fully subsumed by monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stable or increasing short-term rates signals real rate tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over CPIAUCSL alone, or forward returns remain positive when real rates rise above 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect immediate labor market tightening, which boosts short-term consumer confidence and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive accuracy when unemployment falls below 5.0%, or 5-day forward returns show negative mean performance during payroll surges.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate cash flow expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >50% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines, or the signal is statistically indistinguishable from zero after controlling for interest rates.
